---
title: "GEN242: Data Analysis in Genome Biology"
subtitle: "Graduate course — University of California, Riverside"
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

This course introduces algorithms, statistical methods and data analysis
programming for genome biology. It consists of three main components: lectures,
hands-on practicals and student course projects. Students develop practical
skills in reproducible research, statistical analysis, and large-scale
genomics.

## Course components

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-6 .g-col-md-4}
### [Slides](slides/index.qmd)
Lecture slide decks organized by topic, from genome fundamentals through
advanced NGS workflows.
:::

::: {.g-col-6 .g-col-md-4}
### [Tutorials](tutorials/index.qmd)
Step-by-step computational tutorials with executable R and Python code,
interactive tables, and figures.
:::

::: {.g-col-6 .g-col-md-4}
### [Assignments](assignments/index.qmd)
Eight homework sets and a semester project covering the full bioinformatics
analysis pipeline.
:::

:::
